steps.title: Identify the unexpected demand
steps.content: The key signal is "buyers who hadn't previously wanted to purchase old action figures thronged the market"—new demand suddenly appeared. The blank must describe how this happened.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Substitute each choice: rising prices had the effect of ______ demand from buyers who hadn't previously wanted to buy.

A) satisfying — "satisfying demand" means meeting existing desire. But the buyers had no prior desire — demand was nonexistent, then appeared. Opposite of what happened. Eliminate.

B) capitalizing — "capitalizing demand" is grammatically unidiomatic; you capitalize on something, not demand itself. Eliminate.

C) exploiting — "exploiting demand" means taking advantage of existing demand. Again, this demand did not exist before — it was newly created. Eliminate.

D) eliciting — "eliciting demand" means drawing out or calling forth demand that wasn't there before. Buyers who "hadn't previously wanted" now thronged the market — demand was brought into existence. Keep.

D is correct.
